input_text: I am confused between job and higher studies because my family needs money
target_output: Because your family needs financial support, starting with a stable job while gradually preparing for higher studies may be the most practical decision. This reduces immediate financial pressure while protecting your long-term academic goals.

input_text: I am stressed about exams and cannot focus properly
target_output: Your studies should remain the priority right now. Create a realistic study timetable, reduce distractions, and take small daily goals instead of thinking about everything at once. Consistency will reduce stress and improve confidence.

input_text: Relationship stress is affecting my studies badly
target_output: Emotional stability is important before academic performance improves. Take time to organize your thoughts, speak to a trusted mentor or counselor, and protect your mental peace so your studies do not suffer further.

input_text: I want to study abroad but finances are weak
target_output: Higher studies abroad are still possible with proper planning. Focus on scholarships, entrance preparation, and financial planning step by step instead of giving up early. Strong preparation can create opportunities even with limited finances.

input_text: I feel mentally exhausted and confused about my future
target_output: Before making major life decisions, emotional clarity is necessary. Slow down, reduce pressure, and talk to a mentor, counselor, or trusted person. Better decisions come when your mind is stable and clear.
